onboarding.intro_description: Your credentials are encrypted locally; only your approved devices can unlock them. To start, choose where to store your vault.
onboarding.local_description: Encrypted vault stays in browser storage on this device.
onboarding.github_description: Store and sync your encrypted vault securely in a private or public GitHub repository.
onboarding.another_provider: Another encrypted vault file on a different provider.

login.connect_prompt: Connect to storage, then get access.
login.scan_qr: Or scan enrollment QR code from an active device
login.join_request_sent: Join request sent. Please approve on an active device.
login.finish_onboarding_desc: Enter your vault password to finalize enrollment and generate keys.
login.join_instructions: Scan a QR code or paste an enrollment link from a device that is already unlocked. The QR carries provider access and a vault password entry id — enter the password on this device to finish onboarding.
login.enroll_qr_desc: Adds this browser as a trusted device — no approval round-trip.
login.paste_desc: Enter the vault password for the entry referenced in the QR.
login.paste_placeholder: Paste enrollment link or code here…
login.password_placeholder_qr: Vault password for this onboarding QR
login.onboarding_card_desc: This device scanned an onboarding QR. Enter the vault password to pull the vault from the sync provider into local storage and unlock.
login.password_entry_placeholder: Password for the vault entry shown in the QR
login.password_help_text: The password is not stored in the QR. Provider credentials are applied automatically after you enter it.
login.create_vault_subtitle: Your encrypted vault lives on this device. Choose a master password to protect it.
login.create_vault_description: This password unlocks your vault on this device. Nook never sees it — encryption happens locally in your browser.
login.password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
login.local_vault_description: Enter your master password to unlock the vault stored on this device.
login.sync_after_unlock: Cloud sync providers can be connected in Settings after you unlock.
login.use_storage_provider: Use a cloud storage provider instead

settings.title: Settings
settings.storage_desc: Optional cloud copies of your vault
settings.passwords_desc: Passwords available for unlock and device onboarding
settings.devices_desc: Enrolled browsers and pending access requests
settings.remove_provider_confirm: Are you sure you want to remove this provider?
settings.qr_instructions: Scan this code on another device to enroll it automatically into this vault.
settings.password_setup_desc: Set a vault password to allow unlocking without device keys.

help.title: Nook Architecture & Help
help.subtitle: How Nook keeps your vault encrypted and under your control.

validation.github_pat_empty: Enter a GitHub personal access token to connect.
validation.oauth_access_token_empty: Sign in with Google to connect Google Drive.
validation.github_repo_length: GitHub repository name must be 100 characters or fewer.
validation.github_repo_chars: Repository name may only contain letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, and underscores.
validation.drive_file_name_length: Drive vault file name must be 100 characters or fewer.
validation.drive_file_name_chars: Drive vault file name may only contain letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, and underscores.
validation.secret_id_reserved: Secret id cannot use a reserved device id.

errors.google_sign_in_required: Sign in with Google before unlocking.

vault.decryption_failed: Decryption failed. Please check your password.
vault.connection_failed: Failed to connect to storage provider.

vault_passwords.title: Onboard another device
vault_passwords.desc: Generate a QR/link that carries provider access and a vault password so another browser can join this vault.
vault_passwords.warning_banner: Create a vault password to unlock from another browser or generate an onboarding QR from the Onboard page. Use a long, unique value.
vault_passwords.info_desc: These passwords can unlock the vault and can be selected on the Onboard page when you generate a QR/link for another device.
vault_passwords.label: Label
vault_passwords.remove_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this password? Enrolled devices using this password will not be affected, but you won't be able to onboard new devices with it.
vault_passwords.min_length_error: Password must be at least 5 characters.
vault_passwords.enter_pw_error: Enter the password for this entry to issue a code.
vault_passwords.remove_body_suffix: ? Other passwords stay active. If this is the last password, the vault returns to device-key unlock for this browser.
vault_passwords.issue_desc_suffix: to generate a QR/link for the new device.
vault_passwords.scan_qr_desc: Scan this QR with the new device to open Nook, or copy the link.

onboard_device.title: Onboard Device
onboard_device.desc: Generate a QR that points a new browser at a sync provider copy of this vault. The new device downloads the vault locally, then unlocks with the vault password you share separately.
onboard_device.no_sync_providers: No sync providers connected. Add GitHub or Drive in Settings first.
onboard_device.choose_and_verify: Choose a password entry and verify it below.
onboard_device.ready_desc: Scan this QR code from the login screen on your other device, or copy the link.
onboard_device.missing_password_hint: Create a vault password before onboarding another device.

auth_storage.configure_desc: Configure where your encrypted vault file resides.
auth_storage.no_providers_saved: No providers saved. Create or connect a vault below.
auth_storage.confirm_remove: Remove "{label}" from saved providers?{signedOutNote} Your vault file on storage is not deleted.
auth_storage.signed_out_note: You will be signed out of the vault in this browser.
auth_storage.setup_desc: Connect and save this provider in this browser. Only the active provider is used for sync until you switch.
auth_storage.choose_desc: Pick where to store another encrypted vault file. Each provider can point at a different vault.
auth_storage.switch_providers_desc: Switch providers without changing device keys or backup passwords.
auth_storage.sync_providers_desc: Your vault lives on this device. Connect cloud providers to keep an encrypted copy in sync.
auth_storage.sync_choose_desc: Pick a cloud provider to mirror your vault. The local copy on this device stays canonical.
auth_storage.sync_setup_desc: Connect this provider and reconcile with your local vault using version sync.
auth_storage.sync_up_to_date: Already up to date with this provider.
auth_storage.sync_conflict: Versions match but content differs — choose which copy to keep.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_desc: This device and {provider} both report version {version}, but the encrypted content differs.
auth_storage.sync_conflict_banner: Sync conflict with {provider} — resolve before editing secrets.
auth_storage.sync_blocked_edits: Resolve the sync conflict before changing secrets.

pending_joins.instructions: Approve here or manage requests in Settings → Devices.

add_secret.seed_wordlist_error: Could not load the official BIP39 wordlist.
add_secret.choose_type_desc: Choose a type — the form shows only the fields you need.

status_bar.sync_tooltip_github: Synchronize latest changes with your storage provider
status_bar.refresh_tooltip_local: Reload latest changes from browser storage
status_bar.sync_all_aria: Sync vault to all connected providers
status_bar.sync_all_tooltip: Push local vault to every connected sync provider

toasts.device_approved: Your device was approved. Click Connect vault.
toasts.device_removed: This device was removed from the vault.
toasts.vault_created: Created a new vault on this device.
toasts.enrolled_connected: Enrolled and connected to the vault.
toasts.github_connected: Connected to GitHub. Encryption key is stored locally in this browser.
toasts.google_drive_connected: Connected to Google Drive. Encryption key is stored locally in this browser.
toasts.device_enrolled: Enrolled this device via enrollment code.
toasts.error_timeout: Connection timed out. Check your PAT, network, and try again.
toasts.error_unlock_required: Unlock the vault before adding a password.
toasts.password_added_rotate: Added password. Please rotate the genesis password envelope on your other devices if you intend to unlock them with this password.

login_wizard.connection_desc: Connect to storage, then get access.
login_wizard.unlock_desc: Enter vault password or scan enrollment QR.
login_wizard.no_providers: No providers saved. Set up one below.
login_wizard.scan_instructions: Scan QR from an active device to enroll this device automatically.
login_wizard.confirm_enroll_desc: Confirm password to finalize enrollment and generate keys.
login_wizard.enroll_warning: Wait, this device is not yet enrolled.
login_wizard.enroll_choose: Choose a way to enroll it:
login_wizard.enroll_scan_qr: Scan enrollment QR code from an active device
login_wizard.connection_subheader: Pick where your vault file lives
login_wizard.choose_store_vault: Choose where to store your vault
login_wizard.add_provider_desc: Pick another place for an encrypted vault file. Each saved provider is a separate vault location in this browser.
login_wizard.before_connect_desc: Before you can connect, add a storage provider — local device or GitHub.
login_wizard.manage_providers_desc: Add or remove saved providers. Vault files on storage are not deleted.

remote_recovery.title: Vault file missing on storage
remote_recovery.desc_with_cache: The encrypted vault file is no longer on your remote storage, but this browser still has a local copy from a previous session. Recover it to recreate the file on storage.
remote_recovery.desc_missing_only: The encrypted vault file is no longer on your remote storage, and this browser has no local copy to restore. You can create a new vault file from scratch — this replaces what was there before.

join_enrollment.desc_join: This browser is not enrolled yet. Join links your device to the vault without a central nook account.
join_enrollment.desc_pending: Your join request was sent. Try unlocking again after an enrolled device approves you.
join_enrollment.explainer_item1: Join is required so only trusted browsers receive vault keys — reading the encrypted file from GitHub is not enough.
join_enrollment.explainer_item2: Send a request; an enrolled device approves it under Vault settings.
join_enrollment.explainer_item3: Approval wraps the vault keys for this browser’s public key — no plaintext secrets are shared.
join_enrollment.explainer_item4: More enrolled devices mean more recovery paths if one browser is lost.
join_enrollment.have_transfer_keys: Have transfer keys from another device?
join_enrollment.transfer_keys_desc: Paste the two keys copied from an enrolled device. This skips the approval step — only use if you already received them out of band.
join_enrollment.setup_from_scratch_desc: If you cleared this browser and remote storage, create a new vault here. This replaces any existing vault file with a fresh one for this browser.
join_enrollment.approve_on_enrolled: Open Nook on an enrolled device, approve this browser in Vault settings, then unlock again here.
join_enrollment.starting_over_desc: Create a new vault if you reset storage and no longer have an enrolled device to approve this browser.

product_intro.device_is_key: Your vault lives on this device
product_intro.no_master_pw: Encrypted locally with your master password. Optional cloud sync after unlock.
product_intro.bullet2: Your secrets. Your storage. Your keys.
product_intro.bullet3: A decentralized vault for your secrets.

provider_picker.this_device_desc: Encrypted vault in browser storage — no provider login
provider_picker.github_desc: Sign in to GitHub — sync encrypted vault to your repo
provider_picker.google_drive_desc: Sign in with Google — sync encrypted vault to your Drive app data

provider_setup.github_pat_desc: Nook needs a classic personal access token (ghp_) with repo scope to sync your vault file.
provider_setup.vault_file_desc: Vault file: nook-vault.yaml in this repo under your account.
provider_setup.drive_vault_file_desc: Each name is a separate encrypted vault in your Google Drive app data folder.
provider_setup.stored_locally_desc: Stored in this browser only. Syncs to username/{repo}/nook-vault.yaml.
provider_setup.local_storage_desc: Your vault is stored in IndexedDB on this device. No token needed.
provider_setup.google_drive_desc: Nook stores your encrypted vault in a hidden Google Drive app folder. Click Sign in with Google — no API keys to copy.
provider_setup.google_tokens_local_desc: OAuth tokens stay in this browser only, like a saved GitHub token.
provider_setup.google_oauth_unconfigured: Google sign-in is not configured. Set GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID in google-oauth-config.ts.

devices_card.single_device_warning: Add another device before removing this one. A backup password helps with recovery, but at least one device key must remain enrolled.
devices_card.no_devices: No enrolled devices found for this vault.
devices_card.confirm_revoke_current: Remove this browser from the vault and lock it?
devices_card.confirm_revoke_other: Remove this device from future vault access?

app.github_aria: Nook is open source on GitHub
